heroHeadline: Drone Strike on Kutum Wedding Kills 30+ Including 17 Children; Berlin Preparatory Talks Underway in Addis Ababa
heroSubtitle: A drone strike on a wedding ceremony in Kutum, North Darfur on April 10 killed at least 30 people including 17 children — UN Secretary-General condemned the attack, calling aerial strikes on civilians 'unacceptable'; Berlin Conference preparatory talks in Addis Ababa entered Day 2 (April 10–12) as Amnesty International called on high-income countries to use the gathering to 'save lives' as the conflict approaches its three-year anniversary on April 15; UN reports 14 million displaced — 9 million IDPs and 4.4 million refugees — the world's largest displacement crisis, with Sudan's 2026 humanitarian plan only 16% funded against a $2.9 billion requirement
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews
